caption: Graphic shows common (red) and less common (yellow) sites of Charcot joints. Etiology is suggested by location.
downloadDecisionReason: location-etiology map; opening organizer
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer map as canonical; archive STATdx exact duplicate.

caption: Sagittal T1WI MR, part of a series in a patient with diabetic Charcot foot, shows numerous abnormalities. Charcot joints are noted at navicular cuneiforms . There is ↓ signal in the plantar soft tissues, with confluent ↓ signal in the adjacent cuboid , concerning for osteomyelitis. It also shows diffuse ↓ signals throughout the talus , an unexpected finding.
usedFor: Charcot foot vs osteomyelitis MR problem solving
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient MR problem-solving series: Charcot joints, cuboid osteomyelitis/sinus tract, talar insufficiency fracture, and talar osteonecrosis on adjacent slice.
downloadDecisionReason: Charcot foot vs osteomyelitis MR problem solving
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer atomic diabetic-foot MR cluster RP-02..RP-04; retain STATdx alternate cluster as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Sagittal STIR MR in the same patient further defines the talar abnormality as an insufficiency fracture of the dome .
usedFor: talar insufficiency fracture in diabetic foot
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient MR problem-solving series: Charcot joints, cuboid osteomyelitis/sinus tract, talar insufficiency fracture, and talar osteonecrosis on adjacent slice.
downloadDecisionReason: talar insufficiency fracture in diabetic foot
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer same-patient cluster; retain STATdx alternate as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Sagittal T1WI C+ FS MR, adjacent slice, shows a wedge-shaped region of hypointensity , representing a segment of osteonecrosis (ON) in the body of the talus. Remember that the talus is prone to ON and that the tarsal bones are prone to insufficiency fracture in diabetic feet. Sinus track led to the cuboid, confirming osteomyelitis, which was suspected based on both T1 and STIR.
usedFor: talar osteonecrosis and sinus tract/osteomyelitis clue
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient MR problem-solving series: Charcot joints, cuboid osteomyelitis/sinus tract, talar insufficiency fracture, and talar osteonecrosis on adjacent slice.
downloadDecisionReason: talar osteonecrosis and sinus tract/osteomyelitis clue
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer adjacent-slice talar ON/infection problem-solving cluster intact; retain STATdx alternate/adjacent-slice examples as recognition reinforcement.

caption: AP radiograph shows swelling about the tarsometatarsal joints . Careful observation reveals fragmentation at the base of the 3rd metatarsal, with likely fragmentation of the bases of the 4th and 5th as well. There was no history of trauma; this is a Charcot foot.
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient radiograph suspicion and CT proof of metatarsal-base fragmentation/subluxation.
downloadDecisionReason: TMT Charcot radiographic suspicion
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer radiograph/CT TMT cluster; add STATdx Lisfranc/TMT cluster as primary supplement, not replacement.

caption: Short-axis CT, same patient, proves the fragmentation and mild subluxation of the metatarsal bases ; it is often far more severe than suspected on the basis of radiographs. As one would expect, the patient is diabetic.
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient radiograph suspicion and CT proof of metatarsal-base fragmentation/subluxation.
downloadDecisionReason: CT proof of TMT fragmentation/subluxation
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer CT proof with RP-05; add STATdx CT as separate supplemental diabetic-foot example.

caption: Lateral radiograph shows an old and healed calcaneal insufficiency avulsion fracture , typical in diabetics. The talonavicular joint shows concavity of the articular surface of the talus, along with some sclerosis , suspicious for early Charcot joint.
caseClusterNote: Atomic follow-up pair: early suspected talonavicular Charcot progresses 2 months later to classic debris and distended capsule.
downloadDecisionReason: early talonavicular Charcot
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer talonavicular early/progression pair RP-07..RP-08; retain STATdx alternate pair as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Lateral radiograph of the same foot 2 months later shows ongoing destruction at the talonavicular joint . Debris has migrated dorsal to the talus, contained within a distended joint capsule . This is a classic Charcot joint.
caseClusterNote: Atomic follow-up pair: early suspected talonavicular Charcot progresses 2 months later to classic debris and distended capsule.
downloadDecisionReason: 2-month progression with dorsal debris
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer follow-up pair; retain STATdx alternate pair as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Sagittal T1WI C+ FS MR in a Charcot foot shows fluid collections (some containing debris) surrounding the ankle and abnormal signal within the distal tibia . Metallic artifact obscures the subtalar, talonavicular, and calcaneocuboid joints.
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient MR fluid collections/reactive enhancement, biopsy negative for infection.
downloadDecisionReason: fluid collections mimicking abscess
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer because it teaches neuropathic fluid collections/reactive change vs infection.

caption: Axial T1WI C+ FS MR in the same patient shows fluid collections in the soft tissues and tendon sheaths . These are neuropathic fluid collections. Bones enhance, related to reactive change. There was no infection at biopsy.
usedFor: reactive enhancement and biopsy-negative infection mimic
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient MR fluid collections/reactive enhancement, biopsy negative for infection.
downloadDecisionReason: reactive enhancement and biopsy-negative infection mimic
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer with RP-09 because same-patient biopsy-negative infection mimic cluster.

caption: AP radiograph shows a dislocated shoulder with severe fragmentation and bony debris. The placement of the debris suggests massive distension of the glenohumeral joint and subacromial/subdeltoid bursa . The combination of findings is typical of Charcot (neuropathic) shoulder.
caseClusterNote: Atomic shoulder Charcot plus cervicothoracic syrinx etiology proof.
downloadDecisionReason: Charcot shoulder with massive effusion/debris
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer shoulder radiograph + syrinx pair; retain STATdx shoulder example as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Sagittal T2 MR, same patient, shows a large cervicothoracic syrinx . Syringomyelia is the most common etiology of Charcot changes in the glenohumeral joint.
caseClusterNote: Atomic shoulder Charcot plus cervicothoracic syrinx etiology proof.
downloadDecisionReason: syringomyelia etiology proof
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer syrinx proof; retain STATdx syrinx proof as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Lateral radiograph of a paraplegic shows a burst fracture of L1 treated with partial corpectomy, strut graft , and posterior rods. Note the osseous destruction and instability at the next 2 levels . These Charcot vertebrae are typically located immediately caudad to the stabilized spine.
usedFor: Charcot spine after spinal cord injury/stabilization
caseClusterNote: Atomic paraplegic Charcot spine pair at levels caudad to stabilization.
downloadDecisionReason: Charcot spine after spinal cord injury/stabilization
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer paraplegic spine pair; retain STATdx spine example as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Lateral radiograph shows significant subluxation and destruction of endplates with osseous debris at 2 adjacent levels . This paraplegic patient developed instability and Charcot spine.
caseClusterNote: Atomic paraplegic Charcot spine pair at levels caudad to stabilization.
downloadDecisionReason: spinal destruction/subluxation/debris
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer paraplegic spine pair; retain STATdx spine example as recognition reinforcement.

caption: AP radiograph shows ragged destructive changes of the femoral condyle and osseous debris in a young patient. His contralateral AP knee had similar abnormalities.
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient knee radiograph/MR demonstrating debris, distension, destruction.

coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer congenital-insensitivity knee pair; retain STATdx knee example as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Axial T2WI MR in the same patient shows a large effusion and 1 of multiple pieces of osteocartilaginous debris . This patient's knees show the debris, distension, and destruction typical of neuropathic joints. The etiology in this case is congenital insensitivity to pain.

caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient knee radiograph/MR demonstrating debris, distension, destruction.

caption: AP radiograph shows an unusual case of neuropathic joint, resulting from congenital indifference to pain. The left knee shows severe disruption with subluxation, fragmentation, attempted repair, and complete destruction of the joint.
caseClusterNote: Atomic contralateral limb/polyarticular lower extremity example.
downloadDecisionReason: polyarticular congenital indifference lower-limb destruction
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer polyarticular congenital-indifference example; add STATdx knee/ankle examples as primary supplements.

caption: Lateral radiograph of contralateral limb in same patient shows just as severe disruption with destruction of the tibiotalar, subtalar, and midfoot joints. Congenital indifference to pain is an unusual cause of Charcot joint and is often polyarticular.
caseClusterNote: Atomic contralateral limb/polyarticular lower extremity example.
downloadDecisionReason: contralateral ankle/midfoot destruction
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer contralateral limb pair; add STATdx atrophic ankle as separate primary supplement.

caption: AP radiograph in a middle-aged patient gives the initial impression of uncomplicated osteoarthritis, with cartilage loss , large subchondral cyst formation , superolateral subluxation of the hip, and calcar buttressing .
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient hip time-lapse plus cirrhosis etiology proof; do not omit RP-22.
downloadDecisionReason: hip Charcot mimicking osteoarthritis initially
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer hip time-lapse/alcohol-cirrhosis cluster RP-19..RP-22; retain STATdx alternate hip progression example as recognition reinforcement.

caption: Coronal CT, obtained 2 months later in the same patient, shows severe and rapid progression of destruction of the femoral head and acetabulum. Abundant debris is seen within the joint.
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient hip time-lapse plus cirrhosis etiology proof; do not omit RP-22.
downloadDecisionReason: rapid CT progression within 2 months
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer time-lapse cluster; archive STATdx exact duplicate CT.

caption: AP radiograph in the same patient 7 months later shows the classic hatchet sign of rapid destruction of the hip. The rapidity of the destruction and the appearance suggest a diagnosis of rapidly destructive osteoarthritis of the hip. However, one must also consider Charcot arthropathy as an etiology.
usedFor: hatchet sign and rapid destructive hip
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient hip time-lapse plus cirrhosis etiology proof; do not omit RP-22.
downloadDecisionReason: hatchet sign and rapid destructive hip
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer 7-month hip progression and hatchet sign; retain STATdx 2-month alternate as recognition reinforcement, while clearly source-labeling it to avoid timeline confusion.

caption: Axial CT from the same patient shows a small, nodular liver, typical of cirrhosis. The most common etiology of Charcot hip is alcoholism; the cirrhosis proves the etiology in this case.
caseClusterNote: Atomic same-patient hip time-lapse plus cirrhosis etiology proof; do not omit RP-22.
downloadDecisionReason: cirrhosis proof of alcoholism etiology
coverageDecision: Keep RadPrimer because cirrhosis CT proves alcoholism etiology for Charcot hip.

caption: AP radiograph of the shoulder shows destructive changes within the glenohumeral joint, resulting in a straight cut-off appearance of the humeral head . The humerus is dislocated from the glenoid. There is a huge effusion with debris located within it in the subscapularis recess . These are typical findings of a Charcot shoulder. The patient proved to have syringomyelia.
downloadDecisionReason: shoulder straight cut-off/mass-mimic effusion

caption: Lateral radiograph of the ankle shows a long-term Charcot joint involving both the hindfoot and midfoot. The talus has completely resorbed in the process, leaving the tibia articulating with the morphologically abnormal calcaneus. Additionally, the navicular is without proximal articulation, and the calcaneus is significantly subluxated relative to the cuboid.

caption: AP radiograph of the knee shows fragmentation and a tense effusion, which decompresses down fascial planes of the leg. This decompression carries bony debris distal to the joint . This is a typical Charcot knee in a patient with acquired syphilis.

caption: AP radiograph of the foot shows lateral dislocation of the metatarsals relative to the tarsals. The medial cortex of the 1st metatarsal should align perfectly with the medial cortex of the medial cuneiform. Vascular calcification is present. This is a Lisfranc dislocation resulting from diabetic neuropathy.
